title: The Paradigm
author: Syd Field
source: Screenplay, 1979. Later refined with the Midpoint and two Pinches.

lineage.note: The foundational modern screenwriting text. Everything prescriptive after this is a reaction to or elaboration of Field. Tied to page count (~120 pages = 120 minutes; Plot Point I near p.25, Plot Point II near p.85).

stages.name: Set-Up
stages.function: Establish protagonist, world, and dramatic premise. Pages 1-25.

stages.name: Plot Point 1
stages.function: Page ~25. An event that spins the action into Act II and gives the story its new direction.

stages.name: Confrontation
stages.function: Protagonist faces escalating obstacles. The long middle. Pages 25-85.

stages.name: Midpoint
stages.function: Page ~60. A major reversal or new piece of information that changes the protagonist's relationship to the goal. Flanked by two Pinches (~37 and ~75) that re-apply pressure.

stages.name: Plot Point 2
stages.function: Page ~85. Another event that spins into Act III.

applicability.limitations: Tied to feature-page math; doesn't translate cleanly to TV or to documentaries that don't have a 120-minute target. The midpoint is real but the two Pinches are often forced.
